commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed because this external, maintainer-labeled PR changes a public approval SDK and security-sensitive channel behavior while real behavior proof and hardening fixes are still missing.; Needs attention: No effective dependency package or lockfile delta is present beyond package export metadata, but the new approval prompt helper has an unresolved hardening issue.; Review finding: [P2] Escape approv... (sha=e955a497df2828da2e8252eed9790b0177eb401a)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime` subpath and migrates WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage reaction approval prompt, binding, and target-store code to shared allow-once, allow-always, and deny helpers.

PR surface: Source -196, Tests +385, Docs +2, Config +4, Generated 0, Other +4. Total +199 across 24 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the code-level fallback bug: source inspection and a small `String.prototype.replace` probe show `$&`, `$$`, `$\`` and `$'` approval IDs are rewritten by the new helper. No high-confidence live reproduction exists for the full channel behavior because the PR explicitly did not test real phone-channel reaction round trips.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because this external, maintainer-labeled PR changes a public approval SDK and security-sensitive channel behavior while real behavior proof and hardening fixes are still missing.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): add reaction approval helpers
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Adds a shared `openclaw/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime` subpath for channel reaction approvals.
- Hardcodes the standard reaction decisions: 👍 allow-once, ♾️ allow-always, 👎 deny.
- Migrates WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage approval reaction rendering, binding, normalization, and target storage onto the SDK helper so channel code stops hand-rolling common approval text and emoji behavior.
- Keeps channel-specific routing/auth details in each channel while centralizing common pending prompt and local exec suppression behavior.

Why does this matter now?

- WhatsApp and Signal already needed matching reaction approval semantics, and the old code duplicated prompt construction and reaction interpretation in each channel.

What is the intended outcome?

- Existing channels can support exec/plugin reaction approvals through a common SDK abstraction, with manual `/approve` instructions still included at the end of the prompt.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No channel config shape changes.
- No changes to the release-owned changelog.
- No new live WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage end-to-end proof in this PR; this is an SDK/channel refactor with focused runtime and changed-lane validation.

What does success look like?

- The three migrated channels render standard reaction prompts, resolve reaction decisions consistently, and continue to pass their focused approval runtime tests.

What should reviewers focus on?

- SDK API shape and whether the remaining channel-specific code is only transport/auth/routing-specific.
- Signal's distinction between reaction-capable native delivery and manual fallback delivery.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Maintainer-requested local dev-loop work from the channel approval specs.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: shared SDK support for reaction-based exec/plugin approvals across WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage.
- Real environment tested: Crabbox Linux remote validation plus local focused approval runtime tests in this worktree.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-native.test.ts`
  - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
  - `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
  - `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm build"` (`run_4ba89d1e4838`)
  - `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (`run_70c74102e9e0`)
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): focused Vitest reported 10 files / 121 tests passed; SDK API baseline check passed; export check passed; Crabbox build `run_4ba89d1e4838` exited 0; Crabbox changed check `run_70c74102e9e0` exited 0.
- Observed result after fix: migrated channels build and typecheck through changed gates, and focused approval tests pass with the shared hardcoded reaction bindings.
- What was not tested: live phone-channel reaction round trips for WhatsApp, Signal, or iMessage.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this PR refactors the shared SDK and channel adapters; live phone testing was not run in this dev-loop pass.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): duplicated per-channel reaction prompt/binding code existed in WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-native.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/approval-native.test.ts`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm build"` (`run_4ba89d1e4838`)
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (`run_70c74102e9e0`)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added SDK tests for hardcoded reaction bindings, emoji normalization, prompt payloads, target store behavior, and local exec suppression checks.
- Updated WhatsApp, Signal, and iMessage reaction/native approval tests for shared SDK payloads and ♾️ allow-always behavior.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- During precommit validation, `pnpm check:changed` caught stale test fixture types and one lint issue. Both were fixed before this PR was opened.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added and updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Channels using native reaction approval prompts now share the same reaction prompt wording and include ♾️ allow-always when allowed.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Approval execution behavior is security-sensitive, but this change preserves channel auth/routing checks and centralizes reaction decision handling.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Incorrectly enabling a reaction binding where a channel cannot authenticate the reactor.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The SDK only handles generic reaction decisions, prompt content, and target storage; each channel still owns reactor authorization, route matching, and delivery eligibility.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Await CI and review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- None yet.
